  "text": "The Kubernetes watch event delivery path flows through three hops:\n\n1. **etcd MVCC Store** (repo: sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd):\n   - `server/mvcc/watcher.go`: defines `watchStream` struct (implements `WatchStream` interface) — the core MVCC-level stream that tracks watchers on etcd keys.\n   - `server/mvcc/watchable_store.go`: defines `watchableStore` which creates `watchStream` instances via `NewWatchStream()`.\n   - `server/etcdserver/api/v3rpc/watch.go`: defines `serverWatchStream` — the gRPC layer struct that bridges the MVCC `watchStream` to the gRPC `Watch_WatchServer`, forwarding events to Kubernetes API server clients.\n\n2. **Kubernetes API Server Watch Cache** (repo: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, under staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/):\n   - `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/watch_cache.go`: defines `watchCache` — an in-memory ring-buffer cache of watch events received from etcd, used to serve watches without hitting etcd directly.\n   - `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go`: defines `Cacher` — the top-level caching storage layer that owns the `watchCache`, runs a `Reflector` to pull from etcd, and dispatches events to registered watchers.\n   - `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cache_watcher.go`: defines `cacheWatcher` — per-client watcher that receives events from the `Cacher` and sends them to the client over the watch response stream.\n\n3. **Kubernetes Client-Go Reflector** (repo: sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0):\n   - `tools/cache/reflector.go`: defines `Reflector` struct — the client-side component that issues List+Watch calls to the API server, receives watch events, and populates a local Store (DeltaFIFO queue) for informers and controllers to consume."
}
